requirements.text: Repeat the Scout Oath, Scout Law, Scout Motto, and Scout Slogan from memory.
Explain in your own words what the Scout Oath and Scout Law mean to you.
Describe what the patrol method is and how your patrol is named and organized.
Show the Scout sign, salute, and handshake. Explain when to use them.
Tie the square knot and two half hitches and explain how they are used.
Describe the requirements for earning the Scout rank and how you fulfilled each one.
With your parent or guardian, complete the exercises in the pamphlet 'How to Protect Your Children From Child Abuse: A Parent's Guide'.
Present yourself to your leader, prepared for an overnight camping trip.
Describe the steps in the BSA outdoor ethics standard, Leave No Trace, and the Outdoor Code.
Demonstrate how to start a fire outdoors with a match and extinguish it safely.
Know the rules of safe hiking and explain how to prevent and treat blisters.
Demonstrate the proper care of a kitchen and eating utensils.
Explain and demonstrate the treatment for heatstroke, dehydration, and hypothermia.
Demonstrate tying the taut-line hitch and the sheet bend.
Participate in a flag ceremony.
Do a Good Turn for an hour.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
Go on a troop or patrol overnight camping trip. Sleep in a tent.
Use a map and compass to complete an orienteering course.
Help plan and cook one hot meal and one cold meal for your patrol or a guest.
Show your ability to make a stove or fire, for cooking, using three different methods.
Demonstrate the proper technique for rescuing someone who has fallen through ice.
Demonstrate the proper use of a pocketknife, ax, and saw.
Explain the concept of service to others and participate in a service project of at least 3 hours.
Show that you are current on BSA cybersafety and bullying prevention guidelines.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
Successfully complete the BSA swimmer test.
Help plan, pack, and prepare a meal for your patrol while on a camping trip.
Demonstrate your ability to use a compass and map to travel at least 1 mile.
Visit a place that practices Leave No Trace.
Demonstrate the use of three different kinds of lashings.
Identify four poisonous plants found in your local area.
Help a Scout from another patrol or another unit get started on a Tenderfoot requirement.
Participate in a minimum of 6 hours of approved service projects.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
Be active in your troop and patrol for at least 4 months as a First Class Scout.
Be subject to the Scout Oath and Law.
Earn six merit badges, including four from the Eagle-required list.
Hold a position of responsibility for at least 4 months.
Plan, develop, and give leadership to one service project of at least 6 hours.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
Be active in your troop and patrol for at least 6 months as a Star Scout.
Be subject to the Scout Oath and Law.
Earn five additional merit badges, including three from the Eagle-required list.
Hold a position of responsibility for at least 6 months.
Plan, develop, and give leadership to one service project of at least 6 hours.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
Be active in your troop and patrol for at least 6 months as a Life Scout.
Be subject to the Scout Oath and Law.
Earn a total of 21 merit badges, including the 13 required for Eagle.
Hold a position of responsibility for at least 6 months.
Plan, develop, and give leadership to a service project that is helpful to any religious institution, school, or community.
Participate in a Scoutmaster conference.
Successfully complete a Board of Review.
